properties.name: TOKYO BUNBUN(東京文聞)
properties.description: Optional Fight Choreography experience.

properties.name: teamLab Borderless: MORI Building DIGITAL ART MUSEUM
Imperial Palace Main Gate Ishibashi Ornamental Lamp (13 Assassins, Kubi)
Tama River Area (Adrift in Tokyo)
properties.description: Adrift in Tokyo (Satoshi Miki) Popular location for walking scenes in the film.

properties.name: Roppongi Hills Mori Tower (Tokyo Drift)
properties.description: Fast and Furious: Tokyo Drift (Justin Lin)

properties.name: Tokyo Tower (Godzilla and others)
properties.description:
Godzilla Minus One (2023, Takashi Yamazaki)
And Godzilla Statue!

properties.name: Rikkyo University (Tokyo Ghoul)
properties.description:
Rikkyo University (Kamii University)
The real-life inspiration for Kamii University, where Kaneki and Hide attend.

properties.name: Shinjuku Station East Exit Smoking Area (Tokyo Ghoul)
properties.description:
Shinjuku Station East Exit
This busy hub features in a key scene when Kaneki goes to meet Touka.

properties.name: Kabukichō (Tokyo Ghoul)
properties.description:
Kabukicho Crossing
Known as the nightlife district of Shinjuku, Kabukicho features in multiple scenes of Tokyo Ghoul.

properties.name: Yotsuya Station (Your Name)
properties.description: Featured multiple times in the film, this station is near many key locations.

properties.name: Takeshita Street Entrance Arch (JJK and Tokyo Drift)
properties.description:
Another key spot in Jujutsu Kaisen, where the trio meets Nobara.

Also seen in Tokyo Drift.

properties.name: Edo-Tokyo Open Air Architectural Museum (JJK) (Samurai)
properties.description: JJK: Culling Game; the Zenin clan house

properties.name: Adachi City (Shoplifters)
properties.description: The film captures the quieter, more suburban neighborhoods of Tokyo.

properties.name: Tokyo Station (First Ward)
properties.description:
The 1st Ward is where the CCG Main Office is located, and it serves as the headquarters for anti-Ghoul operations. It’s based on Chiyoda, home to major governmental buildings and Tokyo Station.
Notable Spot: Tokyo Station, a historic station with modern vibes, representing the CCG's power and influence.

properties.name: Roppongi (3rd Ward)
properties.description:
Description: The 3rd Ward features Roppongi, a famous nightlife district and key location in Tokyo Ghoul where Kaneki and Rize go on their fateful date.
Notable Spot: Roppongi Hills, known for its luxury shopping and skyline views, including a perfect view of Tokyo Tower at night.

properties.name: Tokyo Skytree (6th Ward)
properties.description:
Description: In Tokyo Ghoul, the 6th Ward is known for its high-tech vibe, which is mirrored by Taitō’s Tokyo Skytree, the tallest tower in Japan, representing a modern and futuristic Tokyo.
Notable Spot: Tokyo Skytree — a great spot for viewing Tokyo’s cityscape and tying in the "Neo-Tokyo" feel.

properties.name: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building (JJK)
properties.description: Description: This towering building, located in Shinjuku, appears in Jujutsu Kaisen and is an iconic structure in Tokyo. It’s featured when major city-wide incidents unfold in the series.

properties.name: Aoyama Cemetery (JJK)
properties.description: Description: This location serves as the setting for darker moments in Jujutsu Kaisen. Aoyama Cemetery, known for its peaceful setting amidst the city, provides the backdrop for eerie scenes in the show.

properties.name: Yoyogi National Gymnasium (JJK)
properties.description: Description: This landmark appears during the events of the series, representing a key battle arena.

properties.name: Hotel Gajoen Tokyo (Sprited Away)
properties.description: Hotel Gajoen in Tokyo is believed to have inspired the design of the bathhouse in Spirited Away. Known as one of Tokyo's "Museum Hotels," the hotel features stunning, traditional Japanese architecture with lavish interior designs, ornate rooms, and unique artistic displays, closely resembling the detailed design of the bathhouse in the film.

properties.name: NTV Big Clock designed by Hayao Miyazaki
properties.description: Description: A giant mechanical clock designed by Hayao Miyazaki himself, this Ghibli-themed clock is located outside Nippon Television Tower in Shiodome. Every hour, the clock comes alive with a whimsical performance.

properties.name: Suga Shrine (Your Name)
properties.description: The staircase where the characters meet in Your Name is located near Suga Shrine in Yotsuya, Tokyo. This spot has become a pilgrimage site for fans of the movie.

properties.name: Asukayama Park (Spirited Away)
properties.description: Old anime style yellow bus that can be used for scene recreations.

properties.name: Tamagawa Aqueduct Greenway (Garden of Words)
properties.description: Scene: The shaded pathways of the Tamagawa Aqueduct serve as the setting for reflective walks in The Garden of Words.

properties.name: Fujiwara Tofu Shop (Initial D)
properties.description: The Tofu Ship from Initial D.

properties.name: D'z Racing Cafe Garage (Initial D)
properties.description: Racing Cafe from Initial D.

properties.name: Fujiwara Tofu Shop (Initial D)
properties.description: Original Tofu shop and now museum for Initial D.

properties.name: National Diet Building (Shin Godzilla)
properties.description: Shin Godzilla.

properties.name: Wako Namiki-Dori Building (Godzilla Minus One)
properties.description: Godzilla Minus One.

properties.name: Chuo-dori Avenue (Suzume, Tokyo Drift)
properties.description: Seen briefly, especially for night traffic B-roll; actual street footage used in Tokyo Drift.

properties.name: Yokohama Red Brick Warehouse (Godzilla Minus One)
DevilCraft Kanda (Godzilla)
properties.description: Technically,  a filming site, but also a fun pizza place. Located in the Kanda area, Devil’s Craft is a popular American-style craft beer bar known for its pizzas.

properties.name: Maneki-neko Hall (Kubi, 13 Assassins)
properties.description: Known as the “Lucky Cat Temple,” Gotokuji also has historical significance, with architecture and grounds that mirror the serene look of traditional samurai settings. Its quieter ambiance is ideal for contemplative scenes in a historical context.

properties.name: Kurazukuri no Machinami / Warehouse District (Kubi, 13 Assasssins)
Kawagoe Kumano Shrine
properties.description: A small, atmospheric shrine known for its spiritual atmosphere and sacred trees. It’s featured in anime like Tsuki ga Kirei as a local meeting spot. The shrine is popular among locals for its calming presence and traditional rituals.

properties.name: Azabujuban (Tokyo Drift)
properties.description: Exterior of Sean’s dad’s house; demolished, but real location at Mita 1-chome block 10

properties.name: Kawagoe Hikawa Shrine
properties.description:
DandaDan: Used by pilgrims as a thematic stand-in within Kamigoe’s shrine-town atmosphere (links to Kawagoe festival culture seen around Old Town).

Tsukigakirei; as itself.

properties.name: Takazawa bridge
properties.description: A simple yet picturesque bridge featured in Tsuki ga Kirei. It serves as an iconic spot where characters share conversations and quiet moments, capturing the atmosphere of a small Japanese town.

properties.name: Kenryuji
properties.description: A peaceful temple surrounded by greenery, appearing as a calming background in anime and travel shows. Its understated charm and traditional architecture make it a fitting place for contemplative scenes.

properties.name: Japan Post
properties.description: This post office appears in Tsuki ga Kirei when Kotaro posts a letter, showing a mundane yet relatable part of daily life in Kawagoe. It’s an understated but recognizable spot for fans.

properties.name: Tower Records Shibuya
properties.description:
Taki is seen walking in front of this building at the opening.

Movie time: 00h 02m 22s

properties.name: 4-chōme-5-1 Azabujūban (Tokyo Drift)
properties.description: Seen during early scenes at Sean’s new home—visible from the bridge area

Place of the "Yukina-Vision" building, shown in one of the time lapses. Also Taki is seen standing there and stretching his arm towards Mitsuha in the opening.

Movie time: 00h 00m 35s and 00h 32m 00s

properties.name: 35°40'48.9"N 139°43'08.7"E
properties.description:
Mitsuha is seen running in the pedestrian walk over the train line, looking for Taki, at the end of the movie.

Movie time: 1h39m38s

properties.name: 新都心歩道橋
properties.description:
Pedestrian bridge in Shinjuku where Taki and Mitsuha walk cross each other without recognizing, and looking back at the wrong timing.

Movie time: 1h 37m 05s

properties.name: Cafe La Boheme Shinjuku Gyoen
properties.description:
Cafe La Boheme. Real place that inspires the restaurant "Il Giardino delle Parole", where Taki and Okudera worked.

Movie time: 22m 57s.

properties.name: Shinanomachi Sta. Minamiguchi (South Exit)
properties.description:
Shinanomachi Station pedestrian bridge.

Taki and Okudera-sempai dismiss their date; Taki tried to comunicate with Mitsuha by phone in October 3rd, 2016. Movie time: 48m 31s

Mitsuha tried to locate Taki, October 3rd. 2016. Movie time: 1h 13m 49s.

properties.name: Shuto Expressway
properties.description: Akira: Inner Circular Route (used as a basis for Neo-Tokyo motorbike scenes

properties.name: Higashi-Nakano Station
properties.description: Shoplifters:  Residential scenes filmed near here

properties.name: Ueno Zoological Gardens
properties.description: Tokyo Ghoul: some scenes set here (especially related to Kaneki’s early trauma)

properties.name: The National Art Center
properties.description: (Suzume) – Scene with Tamaki

properties.description: Jiro Dreams of Sushi.

properties.name: Sugoi Niboshi Rāmen Nagi Shinjuku Golden Gai

properties.description: Dried fish ramen shop from Ramen Heads.

properties.name: Shuuichi (Ramen)
properties.description: Curry ramen shop from Ramen Heads. A personal favorite.

properties.name: Nerima Station
properties.description: Chainsaw Man, Episode 2. Sea Cucumber Devil sequence

properties.name: Pitat House - Nerimanishiguchi
properties.description: Chainsaw Man; Ep.2 city-street intercut in the same Nerima sequence.

properties.name: Takushoku University Bunkyo Campus

properties.name: Shimazaki Book Binding Works

properties.name: Onnazaka
properties.description:
Since the Edo period, this area, which forms the border between Sarugakucho and Surugadai, has been a cliff, and originally there was no path. 

This is the staircase from Chainsaw Man Reze.

properties.name: Catholic Kanda Church
properties.description: In front of the church is the phone booth from Chainsaw Man Reze.

properties.description: JJK: Culling Game

properties.description: 'THE WOLVERINE' (2013) & 'INTO THE SUN' (2005) - funeral scene & meeting with bad guys!

properties.description: 'JU-ON: THE GRUDGE II' (2003) - The Grudge house & the steps from the finale

properties.description: 'LOST IN TRANSLATION' (2003) - Charlotte visits here in the rain

properties.name: Nakagin Capsule Tower
properties.description: 'THE WOLVERINE' (2013) - The "Hiroshima Love Hotel"

properties.name: Big Apple Slot & Pachinko
properties.description: 'THE WOLVERINE' (2013) - Pachinko Parlour where Logan & Mariko hide out

properties.name: Ueno Station
properties.description: 'THE WOLVERINE' (2013) - Logan & Mariko take a bullet train

properties.name: Adidas Futsal Park Shibuya
properties.description: 'THE FAST & THE FURIOUS: TOKYO DRIFT (2006)

properties.name: Takadanobaba Station
properties.description: 'THE SHINJUKU INCIDENT' (2009) & 'THE WOLVERINE' (2013)

properties.name: Shinjuku Omoide Yokocho (Midnight Diner)
properties.description: The alleyways in Midnight Diner are very similar to the ones found in Omoide Yokocho, a small, narrow street filled with small restaurants serving classic Japanese dishes. This is a common setting for intimate late-night meals, reflecting the tone of the series.

properties.name: Senkaku Junction (JJK)
properties.description: Description: The multi-level highway junction is used for intense fight sequences in Jujutsu Kaisen. Its twisting roads provide a unique urban battle setting.

properties.name: Kabukicho, Shinjuku
properties.description: First Love (Takashi Miike) A significant portion of the film takes place in this entertainment district.

properties.name: Harajuku Station (JJK)
properties.description: A prominent location in Jujutsu Kaisen, where Itadori, Fushiguro, and Gojo meet.

properties.name: Benitsuru
properties.description: FLUFFY Pancakes—a pancake shop that perfectly balances salt and sweetness in its pancakes, making it a representative sweets store for those sightseeing in Asakusa.

properties.name: Kanda Yabu Soba
properties.description: a favorite of mine; a soba restaurant renowned for its traditional Edo-style noodles. The restaurant, rebuilt following a fire, continues to serve various soba dishes, including the Kamo-san ban soba featuring thick slices of duck and the ten-seiro soba paired with crispy shrimp.

properties.name: KEY'S CAFÉ 秋葉原SEEKBASE店
properties.description: A coffee shop within walking distance in Akihabara with wifi and good group seating. Open early and is a good place to study.

properties.name: KOMEDA'S Coffee
properties.description: This nearby coffee shop is open early, offers good breakfast deals, and has spacious booths for group work or studying. It is a great place to get out of the hostel in the morning.

properties.name: Karashibi Miso Ramen Kikanbō Kanda Honten
properties.description:
—(Prepare to wait) popular for its intensely spicy Karashibi Miso Ramen. The interior, characterized by its black paint, taiko music, and festival-style devil masks, adds to the immersive dining experience. You can customize their spice level on a scale of five for both "kara" (chili heat) and "Shibi" (numbing spice from sansho pepper mix), catering to varying spice tolerances.
1. Feel free to challenge my “Oni” title; we can sit side by side if you live that spicy life. 😉

properties.name: Himuka Shokudo
properties.description:
Shokudo (食堂, shokudō) are casual restaurants or cafeterias that serve a variety of inexpensive Japanese dishes. Many of them are mom-and-pop, hole-in-the-wall type restaurants where the locals eat. They are numerous across Japan in urban centers and on the countryside. 

A meal at a shokudo usually cost around 800 to 1500 yen.

properties.name: SUSHIRO Ueno
properties.description: Many sushi plates at Sushiro are priced at just ¥100, making It an affordable and popular option for conveyor belt sushi in Tokyo.

properties.name: Suzukien Asakusa
properties.description: the world's richest matcha gelato with seven intensity levels, catering to matcha newcomers and aficionados. Established through a collaboration between Suzukien, a historic tea shop from 1853, and Nanaya, a matcha sweets factory, this venue is a must-visit for its unique range of matcha gelato flavors.

properties.name: Syabu-Yō
properties.description: An all-you-can-eat shabu-shabu haven starting from 1,539 Yen for weekday lunch, located on the 4th floor of Atre Akihabara 2, directly accessible from Akihabara Station's Showa-Dori Gate. The restaurant utilizes a touchscreen ordering system in English, Chinese, or Korean, catering efficiently to tourists.

properties.name: Tesshin
properties.description: teppanyaki, particularly featuring premium Kuroge Wagyu beef. Their signature dish is the Kuroge Wagyu Steak, priced at 1,380 JPY plus tax, seasoned with salt, kombu, and wasabi, offering a quintessential Japanese flavor profile.

properties.name: Yakiniku King
properties.description: A top-rated all-you-can-eat Yakiniku restaurant in Tokyo, Yakiniku King specializes in Japanese BBQ and offers a wide selection of meats for grilling. It's known for its quality, variety, and value, making it a must-visit for meat lovers. In 2022, it beat other well-known chains like Gyu-Kaku to rank as the number one Yakiniku spot. The restaurant also caters to tourists with English touchscreen menus.

properties.name: Fruits Parlor Gotō
properties.description: —café with sweet fruit parfaits near the old Hanayashiki amusement park. The menu, which changes seasonally, features delights such as grape and melon parfaits during summer, alongside other sweets like pancakes, kakigori shaved ice, and ice cream. It's a perfect spot for those seeking seasonal fruit desserts.

properties.name: Buta-Daigaku Shimbashi
properties.description: —Known for its pork bowls, which cost approximately 600 Yen for a small size, this is a must-visit for savory pork bowl lovers. It is located near Shibuya.

properties.name: Ginza Kagari - Soba
properties.description: — (Prepare to wait) famous for its sophisticated take on chicken ramen. The restaurant's signature dish, tori-paitan, features a creamy, rich broth derived from chicken, which provides a velvety texture and deep flavor.

properties.name: Hobo Shinjuku Norengai
properties.description: located a short 6-minute walk from Odakyu Shinjuku Station or Minami-Shinjuku Station, offers a collection of restaurants housed in traditional Japanese houses. This area provides a more classic and atmospheric dining experience compared to the modern establishments around Shinjuku Station. The term "Noren-gai" translates to "shop curtain town close to Shinjuku," referring to the traditional curtain (noren) that signifies an open shop. These 8 renovated old folk houses host a variety of dining options, not limited to Japanese cuisine.

properties.name: Soba House Konjiki-Hototogisu
properties.description: —(Prepare to wait) a ramen restaurant in Shinjuku, Tokyo, celebrated for its unique and sophisticated flavors. Awarded a Michelin star in 2018, this ramen establishment is renowned for its shoyu soba.

properties.name: Ramen Sugimoto
properties.description: —(Prepare to wait) ramen shop located in Nakano, Tokyo. This shop offers a unique ramen experience that both locals and visitors highly praise. The restaurant is known for its special shio (salt) ramen, which includes a generous topping of chashu pork, a perfectly cooked egg, and homemade wontons.

properties.name: Shiro-Hige’s Cream Puff Factory
properties.description: Nestled in Setagaya, this unique spot Offers Totoro-shaped cream puffs starting at 400 Yen. It is also a spot for Studio Ghibli fans and sweet tooth indulgers.

properties.name: Yakumo Saryō
properties.description: This serene and minimalist setting for a traditional Japanese breakfast was designed by the renowned architect Shinichiro Ogata. It provides a genuine cultural experience through its dining, highlighting Japanese cuisine as an art form involving various cultural elements, from the dishware to the chef’s etiquette. Yakumo Saryo offers a traditional Japanese breakfast called "Asacha," focusing on tea, priced at ¥4,000 (around $37).

properties.name: Hakushū Teppanyaki
properties.description: This establishment shines with its premium Japanese Wagyu Beef prepared teppanyaki-style in Shibuya. It is celebrated for top-notch Kobe beef.

properties.name: Summit
properties.description: A local grocery story with fresh takeaway choices and sales.

properties.name: Kicks Cafe&Bar
properties.description: A great local place to get a burger.

properties.name: Yatai Hamachan
properties.description: Affordable seafood and izakaya-style dining, famous for its fresh fish and sashimi. Sashimi three kinds at 950 YEN (about $7.10)

properties.name: Niku no Ohyama
properties.description: Known for its deep-fried delights, including beef and seafood, with varying prices based on ingredients.

properties.name: Sanzoku Kaizoku
properties.description: known for Takoyaki and Kaisendon, the latter being exceptionally good with various fish trimmings on rice.

properties.name: YAKITORI Nonotori Nihonbashi
properties.description: Yakitori Nonotori in Nihonbashi offers an exceptional dining experience, particularly through its Premium Omakase Course, which features seasonal, expertly grilled skewers and side dishes. The restaurant is praised for its use of binchotan charcoal, which imparts a unique flavor to the yakitori, and its signature tamago kake gohan (rice with eggs cooked in an earthenware pot).

properties.name: Hinoya Curry Akihabara
properties.description: A good introduction to Japanese curry; it is a little spicy.

properties.name: da GIORGIO
properties.description: Brick oven style pizza.

properties.name: HIGUMA Doughnuts
properties.description: HIGUMA Doughnuts in Tokyo Dome City offers fluffy, delicious donuts that are highly praised for their light texture and gentle sweetness.

properties.name: LATTE ART MANIA TOKYO
properties.description: If you're looking for a cool, creative coffee spot, Latte Art Mania is definitely worth a visit. I appreciated the attention to detail in their intricate latte designs. The space is small, but the energy is inviting, and the charcoal latte offers something unique—both in flavor and presentation.

properties.name: Kantan-na Yume
properties.description: Kantan-na Yume feels like a hidden gem tucked away in a quiet part of the city. When I visited, the peaceful atmosphere struck me right away—a nice change from the buzz of Shibuya just minutes away. The wagashi I tried were exquisite, beautifully made, and paired perfectly with a cup of matcha.

properties.name: Homemade Ramen 麦苗
properties.description: f you're looking for an outstanding ramen experience, I highly recommend Homemade Ramen 麦苗. I visited recently, and despite not typically favoring shoyu broths, their version completely won me over with its depth and rich umami flavor. The noodles and toppings were exceptional—the steak in the deluxe toppings was some of the best I've had. Do note that you'll need to arrive early, around 9 or 10 AM, to put your name on the list due to its popularity. But trust me, the wait is worth it.

properties.name: Udatsu Sushi
properties.description: Some of the best Sushi I have ever experienced. Check the prices first.

properties.name: Menya Nishikawa
properties.description: Beef ramen that might change your life.

properties.name: tonkatsu.jp Omotesando
properties.description: Pork that is on a entirely different level.

properties.name: Torisawa 22
properties.description: Yakitori on another level; huge wait lsit.

properties.name: Men Mitsui
properties.description: If you're in Asakusa and craving top-tier ramen, Men Mitsui is a must-visit! This renowned spot near Tawaramachi Station offers an incredible soy sauce ramen with medium-thick, hand-kneaded noodles, which soak up the rich yet elegantly balanced chicken and seafood broth. The char siu is exceptional, with both thick and seared belly cuts that deliver juicy, flavorful bites, and the side options like the char siu rice bowl and homemade sauce katsudon add an extra touch of indulgence. With its relaxed, traditional atmosphere, flexible ticketing system, and thoughtful service, Men Mitsui truly earns its place as one of Tokyo's standout ramen destinations.

properties.name: Ramenya Shima
properties.description: Ramen-ya Shima in Nishi-Shinjuku delivers an extraordinary ramen experience that’s worth every bit of effort to secure a reservation. The rich soy sauce broth has a sharpness and depth that’s perfectly complemented by tender, thin noodles, while the toppings—from soft char siu to runny-yolk eggs—add layers of texture and flavor. Both the soy sauce and salt ramen options are outstanding, with the salt version bringing a subtle, savory punch that lingers beautifully. Paired with the grilled char siu rice, this bowl of ramen stands among Japan's best, making each visit a blissful culinary highlight.

properties.name: Kibun Sushi
properties.description: If you’re looking for top-quality Edomae sushi in Asakusa, Kibun-zushi is a classic spot with over a century of history and a well-earned reputation. Reservations are essential; on my visit, every seat at the counter was filled, mostly with regulars. The atmosphere inside is dim and quiet, with no curtain on the entrance, creating a low-key vibe.

properties.name: Karashibi Miso Ramen Kikanbō Kanda Honten
properties.description: Its spicy, and you're gonna wait. One of the best ramen dishes in Tokyo. Come for my Oni level, if you dare. ;)

properties.name: Mandarake Shibuya
properties.description: Excellent Otaku shop; manga, collectibles, figures, etc.

properties.name: DAIKANYAMA T-SITE
properties.description: One of the best stores for English language Manga, and amazing collections for animated films, i.e., full storyboards of Akira, etc.

properties.name: Akihabara Radio Kaikan
properties.description: One of the better towers.

properties.name: Pokémon Center Mega Tokyo & Pikachu Sweets
properties.description: This is the Pokémon store; absolutely the best one.

properties.name: VenusFort
properties.description: Specializes in Studio Ghibli goods, with a fancy Roman-themed interior that enhances the shopping experience.

properties.name: Shibuya Parco
properties.description:
Nintendo Tokyo (Shibuya PARCO)
Capcom Store Tokyo (Shibuya PARCO)

properties.name: Tokyo Character Street
properties.description: A corridor of small shops dedicated to popular anime franchises, including Pokemon, Gundam, Ultraman, and Studio Ghibli.

properties.name: Toy Sapiens
properties.description: Come get your toys.

properties.name: Kinji Used Clothing Harajuku Branch
properties.description: One of the better thrift shops in Shibuya.

properties.name: Cat Street
properties.description: A full street of high end thrift shops; it is still expensive, but there is the occasional find.

properties.name: Takeshita Street Entrance Arch (Meiji Street)
properties.description: You've probably seen this already.

properties.name: Gashapon Ikebukuro Main Store
properties.description: The largest small capsule store in the world. Over 3000 machines.

properties.name: Takadanobaba Mikado Game Center
properties.description: Legendary spot. You’ll find Street Fighter II, Aliens vs. Predator, Virtual On, and even Daytona USA.

properties.name: Natsuge Museum
properties.description: 70s–80s games, pure nostalgia
